div.head: The Ambassador in France (Wallace) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1582. Have carried out instructions given in Department’s 1374, August 13, 6 p.m. I was assured by PaleologueMaurice G. Paléologue, secretary general of the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs. that France had not the least intention of sending supplies or troops to Poland across German territory. Goeppert,Heinrich Goeppert, president of the German delegation to the Peace Conference. who left recently to go to Berlin, was so informed by Paléologue.
Paléologue repeated that if Germany tried to nullify the provisions of the peace treaty in the east, France would take action on the Rhine. This attitude had been reported already to the Department.
